narration_text: BREAK POINT. This is the save. Our server is off-camera regrouping; his opponent holds the far baseline, waiting. The next point either holds the game or flips it.
authoring_note: Revised from plan A3 - frame grounding shows Player A off-camera 0-8s. No fabricated 'Player A enters court' at t=0. Generic 'our server' pronoun used because specific player identity not verified.

narration_text: Our target is taking the full shot clock. On a break point, tempo matters as much as pace. This walk is the first reset of the telemetry we will track across the clip.
authoring_note: No decimal citation - qualitative only. No ATP rank / serve-velocity references since the actual player identity is not verified.

narration_text: The server steps into frame, racket in hand. The walk itself is ritual - it is how the ritual-entropy signal will know whether his pre-serve routine is holding shape under pressure.
authoring_note: Future-tense on ritual_entropy - the signal has not sampled yet for this player. Hook names the signal without citing a value. G39 compliant.

narration_text: Settling to the baseline. Racket at the hip, back to us, orange strings flashing. We will have toss-variance in centimeters the instant the ball leaves his hand.
authoring_note: Future-tense on serve_toss_variance_cm - unit is cm per G44 fix. No decimal cited. Dropped the 'five-to-six ball-bounces' claim since it is not visible in the 0-11s window.

narration_text: Toss is up. Left arm extended, right arm loading the racket - contact in a fraction of a second. First serve on the save attempt.
authoring_note: Plan A3 cited split_step_latency_ms as hook - REMOVED. split_step_latency is the RETURNER's signal; our server is serving here. Naming it would be technically wrong.
